TEC Lab Remote Desktop Setup

College of DuPage

Quick Navigation

  • Step 1: Set Up Microsoft Authenticator (Do This First!)
  • Step 2: Run the Windows Installer
  • Step 3: Connect to TEC Lab
  • Mac Instructions
  • Troubleshooting
IMPORTANT - Read Before Starting!

You MUST set up Microsoft Authenticator on your phone BEFORE using HP Anyware.

This is a one-time setup. If you skip it or set it up wrong, you cannot connect!

Step 1: Set Up Microsoft Authenticator

1A. Go to the Security Info Website

On your computer, open a web browser and go to:

https://mysignins.microsoft.com/security-info

Sign in with your COD email (example: smithj@dupage.edu)

Click "+ Add sign-in method"

Click "+ Add sign-in method" to begin setup

1B. Select Authenticator App

In the dropdown, select "Authenticator app" and click Add:

Select "Authenticator app" and click Add

1C. Install the App on Your Phone

Download Microsoft Authenticator from your phone's app store:

  • iPhone: App Store
  • Android: Play Store

Make sure the app is by Microsoft Corporation (look for the blue padlock icon).

Install Microsoft Authenticator from your phone's app store

1D. Scan the QR Code

  1. Click Next on the "Start by getting the app" screen
  2. Click Next on "Set up your account"
  3. On your phone, open Microsoft Authenticator
  4. Tap "Scan a QR code"
  5. Point your phone camera at the QR code on screen

Scan the QR code with the Authenticator app on your phone

1E. Approve the Test Notification

Your phone will show a notification asking you to approve. Tap Approve.

On your computer, click Next when it says "Notification approved".

Tap "Approve" on your phone, then click "Next" on your computer

CRITICAL STEP - Do Not Skip!

1F. Set Your Default Sign-In Method to "Notification"

Back on the Security info page:

  1. Click "Change" next to "Default sign-in method"
  2. Select: "App based authentication - notification"
  3. Click Confirm

Click "Change" next to Default sign-in method

Select "App based authentication - notification" - NOT "code"!

Option Works with HP Anyware?
App based authentication - notification YES - Select this!
App based authentication or hardware token - code NO - Will NOT work!
Authenticator Setup Complete!

You only need to do this once. Now proceed to Step 2.

Step 2: Run the Setup (Windows)

Run the Setup File

In the extracted folder, double-click:

2-Run-This-To-Connect.bat

  1. Read the message that appears
  2. Press any key to continue
  3. HP Anyware will install automatically if needed and connect to TEC Lab
Windows SmartScreen Warning

You may see a blue warning: "Windows protected your PC"

  1. Click "More info" (small text link)
  2. Click "Run anyway"

This is normal - the installer is safe COD software.

Step 3: Connect to TEC Lab

3A. Enter Your Credentials

Enter your COD login:

  • Username: Your COD username only (NOT the full email!)
    • Correct: smithj
    • Wrong: smithj@dupage.edu
  • Password: Your COD password

Click Connect.

Enter your COD username (not full email) and password

3B. Request Push Authentication

A screen will appear saying "RADIUS Authentication Required"

  1. Ignore the "PIN + Token" field - leave it empty
  2. Click "Request Push"

Click "Request Push" (ignore the PIN + Token field)

3C. Approve on Your Phone

Your phone will show a notification from Microsoft Authenticator.

Tap Approve.

Tap "Approve" on the notification on your phone

3D. Select a Computer

After approval, you'll see available computers. Click on one to connect.

Click on an available computer to connect

You're Connected!
  • The physical computer in the lab shows a black screen while you use it remotely
  • Save your work to OneDrive! If disconnected, you have 5 minutes to reconnect

You're now connected to a TEC Lab computer!

Next Time

After the first setup, you can connect by:

  • Running 2-Run-This-To-Connect.bat again, OR
  • Search for "PCoIP Client" in the Windows Start Menu

Mac Instructions

Mac Users: The .exe and .bat files are for Windows only. Follow these manual steps.

Mac Setup Steps

  1. Complete Step 1 (Authenticator setup) - same as above
  2. Download HP Anyware for Mac:
    https://dl.teradici.com/DeAdBCiUYInHcSTy/pcoip-client/raw/names/pcoip-client-dmg/versions/latest/pcoip-client_latest.dmg
  3. Open the downloaded .dmg file, click Agree
  4. Drag PCoIPClient.app to Applications

Drag PCoIPClient to Applications folder

  1. Open from Applications folder
  2. When prompted for Accessibility access, click "Open System Preferences", click the lock, and check PCoIPClient.app

Grant Accessibility access to PCoIPClient

Check the box next to PCoIPClient.app

  1. Click the gear icon (top left) > Advanced
  2. Select "Security mode - Low"

Set Security mode to "Low" (required!)

  1. Click Add connection
  2. Enter:
    • Host Address: hpanyware.cod.edu
    • Connection Name: TEC Lab

Add connection with hpanyware.cod.edu

  1. Connect the same way as Step 3 above (enter credentials, Request Push, approve on phone)

Troubleshooting

Problem Solution
"Request Push" doesn't work Your default sign-in must be "notification" not "code".
Go to mysignins.microsoft.com/security-info and change it.
SmartScreen blocks the installer Click "More info" then "Run anyway"
Installation fails Check internet connection. Click "Yes" on admin prompts.
Can't connect Check internet. On campus, use COD-WiFi or ethernet.
"Connect insecurely" error Security mode needs to be "Low". The installer sets this automatically for Windows.
No computers available All TEC Lab computers may be in use. Try again later.

Internet Speed Requirements

QualityAveragePeak
30 fps (standard)5 Mbps50 Mbps
60 fps (smooth)10 Mbps100 Mbps

Test your speed at speedtest.net
